title: Why Octopuses Squirt Ink
script_summary: Exploring the fascinating reasons octopuses use ink as a defense mechanism.

segments.voiceover: Ink is a smoke screen — and sometimes a decoy — for escape.
Octopuses face predators like sharks and eels. Their ink gives them a fighting chance.
When threatened, they release ink that clouds the water.
This ink contains a chemical that can even dull a predator’s sense of smell.
It's not just a getaway tactic; it can also confuse attackers, allowing for a clean escape.
Want to dive deeper into ocean mysteries? Hit subscribe and explore with us!
